steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject is “National Heritage Areas,” which is plural. The prepositional phrase “like the Arabia Mountain National Heritage Area in Georgia” modifies the subject but doesn't change its number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Subject-verb agreement requires a plural verb with a plural subject. “Are” is the only plural form among the choices. The other three — “has been,” “was,” and “is” — are singular verbs that violate this rule. That matches choice B — B is correct.
